$comment: Neutral shipped <sys/time.h> (POSIX) — SEED of the struct-body descriptor surface (the FIRST `structs` consumer). `struct timeval` is two LP64 longs: tv_sec (time_t) + tv_usec (suseconds_t), both i64 → offsets 0 and 8 (no padding). Field types are spelled as their RESOLVED hir-text form (i64), since parseTypeFromText resolves hir-text builtins, not descriptor typedef names. The semantic phase interns the struct + injects `timeval` into the TAG namespace + a field scope, so c-subset `struct timeval tv; tv.tv_sec` resolves and the layout engine derives the offsets from the field sizes. A SUBDIRECTORY descriptor (sys/time.json — resolved by the c6 subdir fix, distinct from a future <time.h> → time.json). This descriptor is the struct-mechanism PROOF; the rest of sys/time.h (gettimeofday + struct timezone) is added in that header's own cycle (the surfaces are additive).
